'''Yoshi Raph-Elan''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
Shortly following his [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|elevation]] to the rank of Knight in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]],<ref name="FF 128">{{FactFile|128|LOU 1-2|Princess Lourdes}}</ref> Raph-Elan was traveling away from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] when his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was shot down over a backwater [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Forced to crash-land on the [[Lourdes' homeworld|barren world]], he managed to activate a rescue beacon before his ship exploded. Gathering himself, he set out to a nearby city in need of assistance. There he found a frantic population, governed by the feared [[Yahk-Tosh]] conqueror, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Gar-Oth]]. After a brief skirmish in the streets with a [[GX1-series battle droid|battle droid]], Raph-Elan discerned the location of the droid's base, and headed there in an attempt to free the people of the town.<ref name="SWR 27" />

As the Jedi ascended the Royal Palace, he came upon a balcony occupied by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Lourdes]], the heir-apparent to the throne of the desolate world. Realizing the man on her balcony was a Jedi, she described how her [[Lourdes' father|father]] was killed by Gar-Oth, and that a vision describing a hero known as the ''Foreseen'' gave her people hope of freedom. As her aid [[Skeeter]] prepared the Princess for a ball that was to be held that night, Lourdes declared that Raph-Elan would accompany her as a servant. Dressed in formal-wear, Raph-Elan was horrified to learn that the dictator planned to marry Lourdes to make his conquering of the planet legal. Revealing himself, the Jedi brandished his lightsaber, only to discover that it had been damaged during his crash. Angered by the Jedi's arrival, Lord Gar-Oth presented his bride-to-be's wedding gift, a [[Goliath (droid)|Goliath droid]]. Ordering the droid to destroy the Jedi, Raph-Elan managed to damage the hulking machine with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], while using wiring from the damaged droid to mend his lightsaber. Defeating the droid, the Jedi discovered Lourdes had killed Gar-Oth with a sword she had taken from his [[Jilruan/Legends|Jilruan]] [[Security Guard/Legends|guard]].<ref name="SWR 27" />

Soon after in the Princess' chambers Raph-Elan asked whether she believed that he was the ''Foreseen.'' She responded by revealing that the ''Foreseen'' was said to be a woman, meaning that she was the individual of prophecy. Revealing that she had feelings for him, Lourdes told him to return to her when he had grown up a bit more. Skeeter informed the two that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] rescue team had arrived, and Raph-Elan departed safely for [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. On board the [[Jedi courier 1101]] ship, Raph-Elan confided in the pilots that he might return one day to Princess Lourdes.<ref name="SWR 27" />

==Personality and traits==
After having recently attained knighthood, Raph-Elan lacked a constant focus vital to a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. However, he was not want for effort, as he constantly reminded himself to focus on the [[Living Force/Legends|Living Force]] and draw from it to gain strength. Compassionate and eager to help others in need, he was quick to step into and assist in the overthrow of a tyrannical regime. Although living in a time when attachment was banned by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], he fell for Princess Lourdes while stranded on her planet. Although he had feelings for her, he stayed true to the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] and returned to the Order without pursuing a romantic affair with the princess.

==Powers and abilities==
[[File:Jedi_fighter.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yoshi Raph-Elan's starfighter.]]
As with many Jedi, Raph-Elan was not only a skilled lightsaber practitioner, but also highly versed in [[Unarmed combat|hand-to-hand combat]]. While trapped on a backwater planet with his broken lightsaber, Raph-Elan was able to defeat a [[GX1-series battle droid]] with his hands. In addition to his strengths as a combatant, he was trained in fighter piloting. While skilled, his starfighter was shot down and destroyed on a desolate world. He also demonstrate some mechanic skills to fix his weapon.

==Equipment==
Elan had fashioned his own [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], he had used a rare orange [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]] in its construction. It featured a standard design with a single blade but emitted an orange color. It was damaged when its wielder crash-landed on a barren planet. It was later repaired using wiring from a [[Goliath (droid)|Goliath droid]]. Elan also flew a starfighter which got shot down.

==Behind the scenes==
Several years after his creation for the Republic comic, author [[Abel G. Peña]] had planned to kill off Yoshi Raph-Elan. Peña intended to place Raph-Elan on [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] Jedi kill list in ''[[Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous]]'', but his appearance was removed by [[Lucasfilm]] before being published.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23609195|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Abel G. Pena Discussion Thread|user=Halagad_Ventor|date=2006-04-08|time=2:23 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170728174543/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-abel-g-pena-discussion-thread.18750535/page-11}}</ref>
